name: cron-job
summary: Schedule and handle a recurring WP-Cron job via a Ready event listener.
description: Scaffolds a Ready listener that schedules a recurring WP-Cron event (if not already scheduled) and registers the action handler that runs when the cron fires.
problem: You need recurring background work (cleanup, sync, periodic refresh) without managing the schedule by hand or duplicating cron registration across activation hooks.
appliesWhen: You need scheduled work running on a recurring interval
Approximate timing is OK (WP-Cron fires only when the site receives traffic, or via a real cron hitting wp-cron.php)
The job is short — anything long should dispatch into PHPNomad tasks
avoidWhen: You need precise, missed-execution-aware scheduling (use a real job queue)
The job is one-time (wp_schedule_single_event is closer)
The job is too heavy for one request (use phpnomad/task to break it up)
tradeoffs: One class plus a listener registration. Schedule registration is idempotent (skipped when already scheduled). For unscheduling, add wp_clear_scheduled_hook(self::HOOK) to a deactivation hook.
outputs: A {name} class implementing CanHandle that schedules and handles the cron
A getListeners() entry mapping Ready to {name}
postApply: Implement run() with the actual work
Add wp_clear_scheduled_hook in a deactivation hook to clean up on uninstall
If recurrence is custom, register the schedule via the cron_schedules filter

name.aiHint: PascalCase. Suffix with Job for clarity.

recurrence.aiHint: Built-in: hourly, twicedaily, daily, weekly. Custom recurrences require registering via the cron_schedules filter.
